toxicity_notes: Nepovirus poaceae is a plant virus, not a plant species. It causes diseases in various grass species (family Poaceae). The 'toxicity' of a virus refers to its pathogenicity, not a chemical toxicity in the way a plant might be toxic.
Generally safe, but some species can cause allergic reactions (pollen) or mild digestive upset if ingested in large quantities. Awns can be physically irritating to skin or mucous membranes.
native_regions: Worldwide, found on every continent except Antarctica

toxicity_notes: Plant pathogen, causes disease in various grass species (Poaceae family). Not toxic to humans or animals directly.
A plant pathogen that causes disease in species of the Poaceae (grass) family, impacting plant health and yield. Not toxic to humans or animals in the traditional sense.

Nepovirus poaceae is a plant virus, not a plant species. It causes diseases in various grass species (family Poaceae) and is not toxic to humans or animals in the conventional sense, though it can severely impact agricultural yields and plant health.
native_regions: Worldwide (where host Poaceae species are grown)
